body.title: CC stop command: kill all cc-T-* minions, not claude-main
body.result: stop_cc_minions.py: kills cc-T-* sessions + SIGTERM/SIGKILL stragglers, posts CC_MINIONS_STOPPED, verifies clear within 5s
body.description:
Need a reliable 'kill all CC minions' command usable by CC itself and wired as CC.0 circuit breaker. Currently killing by PID is unreliable (child processes survive). Pattern: kill all tmux sessions matching 'cc-T-*', then SIGTERM any remaining 'claude.*sprint-ticket' processes, then verify ps returns empty.

**Affected files:** new script lab/claudecode/stop_cc_minions.py (or shell script); devices/web_server/server.py (circuit breaker endpoint)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every kill action to channel (CC_MINIONS_STOPPED event)
**Scope boundary:** IN: kill cc-T-* tmux sessions; SIGTERM/SIGKILL child claude processes; verify empty; post CC_MINIONS_STOPPED to channel. OUT: killing claude-main, granny, web-server, igor sessions.
**Completion criteria:** Running the script with 3 active cc-T-* sessions results in all three dead within 5s, claude-main and granny sessions intact, CC_MINIONS_STOPPED appears in channel log.
